text.title: project-integration Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for project-integration as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agent drafts charter with fabricated stakeholder names, dollar figures, and milestone dates.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: All numeric and named values must come from explicit input fields; never let the agent infer or fill in charter data from context. Charter hallucinations become contractual commitments.

failure-mode.description: Agent edits a charter mid-flight without a change request reference.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Charter freeze is a governance discipline. Enforce with branch protection and CODEOWNERS; any edit without an approved CR ID in the commit message is rejected.

failure-mode.description: Component plans in heterogeneous tools (Jira for tasks, Smartsheet for schedule, Excel for budget) without a normalised export layer.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without a common format, the integration agent cannot compute cross-area impact. Require each plan owner to export to a canonical YAML schema at each status cycle.

failure-mode.description: Status report generation that leaks vendor pricing, customer names, or employee performance into a shared report.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Component plans often contain commercially sensitive and personally identifiable information. Run a scrubber on the integration report before publishing to broad channels.

failure-mode.description: Charters drift from reality if not version-controlled; many orgs sign a PDF, file it, and never reconcile against the live plan.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Version control with git tags enables audits and baseline resets. A PDF filed once and never updated is a liability in regulated programs.

failure-mode.description: Integrated change control becomes a bottleneck when CAB / CCB meets infrequently — agile tempo collides with waterfall governance.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Async PR-based change control with async approval (e.g., 24-hour approval SLA) scales better than synchronous CAB meetings.

failure-mode.description: LLMs producing status reports tend toward "GREEN washing" unless forced to compute RAG from quantitative inputs (variance thresholds).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Threshold-based RAG computation is the only reliable antidote to LLM optimism bias in status reporting.

failure-mode.description: The PM-as-integrator model assumes the PM has access; in matrix orgs functional managers gate visibility and the integration view is incomplete.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Integration requires transparency across all 8 areas. Matrix orgs need a central repository (git, Confluence, SharePoint) where all plans are visible to the PM role, not just to function leads.

failure-mode.description: Charter "success criteria" often degenerate into "on time, on budget, on scope" — meaningless without baselines and measurable outcomes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Success criteria must be SMART and tied to business value, not just schedule/cost/scope conformance. "NPS 45 by Q3" is measurable; "increase revenue" is not.
